properties.description:
Sets the asset's class using either a rank (a number between 1 and 4) or a display name (e.g., "A," "B," "C," or "D"). If both rank and name are provided, rank takes precedence.
* This attribute accepts a single value. It can be locked: When a class attribute is "locked", this attribute will not be affected by any policy.
  * UI: When the user changes the asset class, by default is "locked".
  * API: The user will need to indicate wether they want to "lock" the class attribute.

The ability to lock asset classes ensures that critical classifications remain unchanged during policy updates, thereby maintaining the integrity of asset management processes.

models.name: CustomBaseImageAttributes

columns.name: include_in_recommendations

properties.description:
Whether this image should be recommended as a base image upgrade. 
If set to true, this image could be shown as a base image upgrade to other projects.
If set to false this image will never be recommended as an upgrade.
properties.business_significance: This boolean flag determines the visibility of the image in upgrade recommendations, impacting user decisions on image selection.

columns.description: Indicates whether the custom base image should be recommended for use as an upgrade.

columns.name: project_id

properties.description:
The ID of the container project that the custom base image is based off of.
The attributes of this custom base image are taken from the latest snapshot at the time of creation.
This means that no changes should be made to the original project after the creation of the custom base image,
as new snapshots created from any changes will NOT be picked up.
properties.business_significance: Serves as the primary key, linking the custom base image to its originating project, ensuring that the image attributes are consistent with the project's state at the time of creation.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the project that the custom base image is based on.

columns.name: versioning_schema

properties.description:
The versioning scheme used by images in the repository.

A versioning schema is a system for identifying and organizing different versions of a project. 
It is used to track changes and updates to the project over time, and to help users identify which version they are using. 
A versioning schema typically consists of a series of numbers or labels that are incremented to reflect the progression of versions. 
For example, a versioning schema might use a series of numbers, such as "1.0", "1.1", "2.0", and so on, to indicate major and minor releases of a product. 
A consistent and well-defined versioning schema helps users and tools understand and track the development of a project.
properties.business_significance: Facilitates the identification of image versions, allowing users to understand the progression and changes made over time, which is crucial for maintaining compatibility and stability in projects.

columns.description: The versioning system used to categorize and track different iterations of the custom base image.

properties.semantic_description: The CustomBaseImageAttributes table stores metadata related to custom base images used in project development, specifically focusing on their recommendation status and versioning. This table is essential for managing image upgrades and ensuring that users can track and utilize the correct versions of base images in their projects.
properties.table_purpose: To manage and track custom base images, including their recommendation status for upgrades and the versioning schema used for organization and identification.
properties.business_context: This table is part of a system that manages custom base images for software projects, particularly in environments where image upgrades are common. It supports project teams in making informed decisions about which images to use based on their upgrade recommendations and version history, thereby enhancing project efficiency and reducing the risk of using outdated or incompatible images.

models.description: The CustomBaseImageAttributes table stores metadata related to custom base images used in project development, specifically focusing on their recommendation status and versioning. This table is essential for managing image upgrades and ensuring that users can track and utilize the correct versions of base images in their projects.

columns.name: format

properties.business_significance: Identifies the ecosystem in which the pinning action is applicable, ensuring that the correct versioning rules are applied.

columns.description: The format of the package management system (e.g., pip, npm) being used.

columns.name: package_name

columns.description: The name of the package that is being pinned.

columns.name: pin_version

properties.business_significance: Critical for enforcing the exact version of a package to be used, which helps prevent compatibility issues and application breakage.

columns.description: The specific version of the package that is being pinned.

properties.description:
Pin a package at a specific version. Pinning is a capability not supported
by all ecosystems, which causes a transitive dependency to be pinned at
a specific version with an override.

For example, the `replace` go.mod directive can override package dependency
constraints, forcing a specific version to be used in the application.

Pinning could break an application.

Note: In practice pin actions today are solely used in python pip's packages as pip does not calculate upgrade paths
properties.semantic_description: The PinPackageAdvice table is designed to manage package version pinning within software ecosystems, particularly focusing on scenarios where specific versions of packages need to be enforced to maintain application stability and compatibility.
properties.table_purpose: To store information about package version pinning, allowing developers to specify exact versions of dependencies that should be used in their applications, thereby preventing unintended upgrades that could break functionality.
properties.business_context: This table is relevant in the context of software development and dependency management, particularly in environments where package upgrades can lead to instability. It is particularly significant in Python development using pip, where version pinning is a common practice to ensure that applications run with known, tested versions of their dependencies.

models.description: The PinPackageAdvice table is designed to manage package version pinning within software ecosystems, particularly focusing on scenarios where specific versions of packages need to be enforced to maintain application stability and compatibility.

models.name: PolicyCondition

columns.name: field

properties.description:
field refers to the type of identifier used in the condition of the policy.  The available value is versioned and hierarchical:
- `snyk/asset/finding/v1` : identity of the finding scoped to a Snyk assets (e.g. a repository).
For a given issue, `snyk/asset/finding/v1` can be extracted from the `key_asset` field in the [issues API](https://apidocs.snyk.io/?version=2024-10-15#get-/orgs/-org_id-/issues). 
In addition, finding identities can be extracted from the fingerprints section of the SARIF, which is accessible via the Snyk CLI. More details can be found in the [CLI documentation](https://docs.snyk.io/snyk-cli/scan-and-maintain-projects-using-the-cli/snyk-cli-for-snyk-code/view-snyk-code-cli-results#export-test-results).
properties.business_significance: Essential for defining the scope of the policy condition, allowing for targeted enforcement based on specific identifiers like Snyk asset findings.

columns.description: Identifier type used in the policy condition, indicating the specific aspect of the asset finding being evaluated.

columns.name: operator

properties.description:
Operator for the field to value matching. Currently
only 'includes' is supported, which does an exact
string match on the value.
properties.business_significance: Determines how the condition is evaluated, ensuring that only relevant findings that meet the exact criteria are considered for policy enforcement.

columns.description: The operator used for matching the field to the specified value, currently limited to 'includes' for exact string matches.

columns.name: value

properties.description: The value of the field to match on.
properties.business_significance: Critical for defining the exact criteria that findings must meet to trigger policy actions, thus enhancing the precision of security measures.

columns.description: The specific value that the field is matched against in the policy condition.

It facilitates the creation of policies that govern how vulnerabilities in software assets are identified and managed, thereby supporting organizations in maintaining compliance and reducing security risks.

models.description: The PolicyCondition table is designed to define conditions for policy enforcement within a security management system, specifically focusing on identifying vulnerabilities in assets through structured criteria.

properties.description:
Sets the asset's class using either a rank (a number between 1 and 4) or a display name (e.g., "A," "B," "C," or "D"). If both rank and name are provided, rank takes precedence.
* This attribute accepts a single value. It can be locked: When a class attribute is "locked", this attribute will not be affected by any policy.
  * UI: When the user changes the asset class, by default is "locked".
  * API: The user will need to indicate wether they want to "lock" the class attribute.
properties.business_significance: Critical for asset categorization and prioritization, influencing how assets are managed and reported. The ability to lock this attribute ensures stability in asset classification against policy changes.

models.name: VersioningSchemaCustomType

columns.name: expression

properties.description:
The regular expression used to describe the format of tags.
Keep in mind that backslashes in the expression need to be escaped due to being encompassed in a JSON string.
properties.business_significance: Essential for Snyk to interpret and analyze the versioning scheme, ensuring accurate upgrade recommendations based on the defined patterns.

columns.description: Regular expression that defines the format of the container image tags.

columns.name: label

properties.description:
A customizable string that can be set for a custom versioning schema to describe its meaning.
This label has no function.
properties.business_significance: Provides context for users to understand the purpose of the versioning scheme, although it does not affect functionality.

columns.description: Customizable string that describes the meaning of the versioning schema.

columns.name: type

properties.business_significance: Helps categorize the versioning schemes, allowing for better management and selection of appropriate schemas for different image repositories.

columns.description: Indicates the type of versioning schema being used.

properties.description:
The Custom Schema type is a way for Snyk to understand your company’s container image tag versioning scheme,
enabling Snyk to give more accurate base image upgrade recommendations.

This schema type is essentially a regular expression that groups the different parts of an image’s tag into comparable sections.

If your container image's tags follow a versioning scheme other than Semantic Versioning (SemVer), 
it is highly recommended that you select the "Custom Versioning" schema for your image repositories.
properties.semantic_description: The VersioningSchemaCustomType table is designed to capture and define custom versioning schemes for container image tags, allowing Snyk to provide tailored upgrade recommendations based on the specific versioning patterns used by a company.
properties.table_purpose: To store regular expressions that represent custom versioning formats for container images, facilitating accurate analysis and recommendations for base image upgrades.
properties.business_context: In the context of containerized applications, managing image versions is critical for maintaining security and compatibility. This table supports Snyk's functionality by allowing organizations to define their own versioning schemes, especially when they do not conform to standard Semantic Versioning. This flexibility enhances the accuracy of vulnerability assessments and upgrade recommendations.

models.description: The VersioningSchemaCustomType table is designed to capture and define custom versioning schemes for container image tags, allowing Snyk to provide tailored upgrade recommendations based on the specific versioning patterns used by a company.

models.name: VersioningSchemaSingleSelectionType

columns.name: is_selected

Whether this image should be the recommendation. Only one image can be selected at a given time. Setting this
as true will remove previous selection.
properties.business_significance: This boolean flag ensures that only one image can be recommended at a time, preventing conflicts and ensuring clarity in recommendations.

columns.description: Indicates whether the image is currently selected as the recommendation.

columns.name: type

properties.business_significance: This column helps in categorizing images, which can be useful for filtering and managing recommendations based on different image types.

columns.description: Specifies the type or category of the image.

properties.description:
The Single Selection Versioning Schema allows manual setting of which image should be given as a recommendation.

Only one image can be set as the current recommendation. If no images are set as the current selection, 
no recommendation will be given.

It is recommended to use this versioning schema if your repository's tags aren't supported by the other schemas.
properties.semantic_description: The VersioningSchemaSingleSelectionType table is designed to manage image recommendations by allowing the manual selection of a single image as the current recommendation. This schema is particularly useful when other tagging schemas are not applicable, ensuring that a clear recommendation can be provided based on user-defined criteria.
properties.table_purpose: To facilitate the manual selection of a single image for recommendation purposes, ensuring that only one image is marked as the current recommendation at any given time.
properties.business_context: This table operates within a recommendation system where images are presented to users based on specific criteria. The ability to manually select an image as the recommendation is crucial for scenarios where automated tagging or selection is not feasible. This schema supports businesses in providing tailored recommendations to enhance user engagement and satisfaction.

models.description: The VersioningSchemaSingleSelectionType table is designed to manage image recommendations by allowing the manual selection of a single image as the current recommendation. This schema is particularly useful when other tagging schemas are not applicable, ensuring that a clear recommendation can be provided based on user-defined criteria.

views.name: getSbom
views.statement:
-- API Endpoint: GET /orgs/{org_id}/projects/{project_id}/sbom
-- Response Model: SbomDocument
SELECT * FROM public.SbomDocument

properties.description:
This endpoint lets you retrieve the SBOM document of a software project.
It supports the following formats:
* CycloneDX version 1.6 in JSON (set `format` to `cyclonedx1.6+json`).
* CycloneDX version 1.6 in XML (set `format` to `cyclonedx1.6+xml`).
* CycloneDX version 1.5 in JSON (set `format` to `cyclonedx1.5+json`).
* CycloneDX version 1.5 in XML (set `format` to `cyclonedx1.5+xml`).
* CycloneDX version 1.4 in JSON (set `format` to `cyclonedx1.4+json`).
* CycloneDX version 1.4 in XML (set `format` to `cyclonedx1.4+xml`).
* SPDX version 2.3 in JSON (set `format` to `spdx2.3+json`).

By default it will respond with an empty JSON:API response.

#### Required permissions

- `View Project history (org.project.snapshot.read)`
